verification_gate: Every company object and workflow has an owner, authority path, bounded decision, approval, evidence, rollback or non-action rule, and no agent can self-authorize.

company_object_universe: COMPANY-001-A Company identity (Element Ark Holdings frame)
gate_result.universe_source: Enumerated 1:1 from the COMPANY-001 goal noun set 'identity, authority, priorities, projects, departments, roles, decisions, budgets, risk, compliance, and proof workflows' — 11 governed objects/workflows, each assigned exactly once.

per_clause_coverage.owner: present for all 11 — a single accountable node named for each (David for identity/budget-final; David/Constitution for authority; NOA/CECCA for priorities/departments; CECCA for projects-delegation/roles/decisions; simulator_agent+Runtime Kernel for risk; Runtime Kernel+David for compliance; verifier_agent+flexnetos_runner for proof).
per_clause_coverage.authority_path: present for all 11 — each traces the canonical chain David -> Constitution -> Board -> NOA/CECCA -> Executives -> Departments -> Workers -> Tools (Master Plan §1), resolved through the 02_AUTHORITY_GRAPH.md v0 narrow graph with delegates_to/escalates_to/verifies/constrains edges.
per_clause_coverage.bounded_decision: present for all 11 — each decision is scoped (e.g., <=3 active priorities; projects must link >=1 priority and carry gates; budgets are forecast-only Resource Burn; decisions carry unblock+rollback; simulation emits constraints not advice).
per_clause_coverage.approval: present for all 11 — each names its approver and the automatic Needs-Human-Approval triggers (spend/legal/external/production/account/credential/trading/irreversible) per Execution gates §11; David is terminal.
per_clause_coverage.evidence: present for all 11 — each names a concrete evidence surface (Master Plan §1.5, 02_AUTHORITY_GRAPH.md invariants, task_graph.source.csv proof_uri, CAP-001 proof, authority_integrity_report.json, ATAS Resource Burn, SHAKE256 witness chains, proof_ledger.jsonl).
per_clause_coverage.rollback_or_non_action: present for all 11 — each states either a rollback (restore prior plan/graph/assignment; ProofRecord rollback_point) or an explicit NON-ACTION rule (no entity creation, no spend, no legal commitment, no operational department, discard failed COW branch).

no_agent_can_self_authorize.how: Each of the 11 objects carries a self_authorization_prevented clause, and the family records five cross_cutting_invariants. Every completion or escalation crosses a boundary to a higher authority (CECCA -> NOA -> David) or to an independent verifier_agent. Root invariants: a task cannot self-verify completion (02_AUTHORITY_GRAPH.md invariant 4); no agent receives unlimited authority (Master Plan §1); the CEO agent may propose/prioritize/delegate but may not execute raw shell commands directly and David is final human authority (Constitution §6); recommendation may not bypass proof (invariant 6).
boundary_examples: Identity/company frame: David-only; NOA/CECCA subordinate to LifeOS and David.
Priorities: no agent adds a 4th active priority or self-elevates work without David.
Projects: a project cannot self-verify; requires verifier_agent + resolved proof_uri.
Roles: an agent cannot grant itself a role/capability; missing capability escalates to CECCA then NOA.
Budgets: no agent authorizes spend (Constitution hard gate); forecast-only until David approves.
Risk: simulation output may not execute directly nor rewrite baseline (RFC-001); MinCut severs hallucinating agents.
Compliance: a claim without a witness-chain/causal root is rejected — bluffed self-certification is mathematically impossible.
Proof: a task cannot self-verify; a separate verifier_agent gates completion.

gate_result.v0_boundary_respected: Full company hierarchy and agent ecology are post-v0 (RFC) per 00_NORTH_STAR.md and Master Plan §2; departments/roles are defined as governance contracts, not instantiated. Explicit deferrals (full org chart, department staffing, generalized multi-company delegation) are honored (07_MVP_VERTICAL_SLICE.md).

store_001_alignment.durable_company_records: Every durable company macro-state record (identity, authority graph, approved priorities, project rows, department/role/capability bindings, decision ledger, budget/Resource-Burn ledger, risk Scoped-Lessons/Guardrails, compliance witness-chain references, proof-ledger references) is owned canonically by PostgreSQL + RuVector (STORE-001 'durable relational macro-state').
store_001_alignment.transient_governance_state: redb holds only transient arbitration/simulation scratch (transient tier); never a second durable writer.
store_001_alignment.per_agent_cognition: AgentDB (.rvf) holds NOA/CECCA/executive/worker cognition (MicroLoRA + SONA), non-authoritative for shared company truth, witnessed by SHAKE256 chains rooted in PostgreSQL source vectors.
store_001_alignment.secrets: OS keyring holds budget/compliance credentials; PostgreSQL stores references/provenance only; redb never holds secrets.
store_001_alignment.invariant_parallel: STORE-001 single-durable-writer (PostgreSQL owns every durable class exactly once) mirrors the governance invariant that no agent self-authorizes: durable company truth has exactly one canonical writer, just as every governed object has exactly one accountable approver.

non_action_verification.spend: none (budgets are forecast-only Resource Burn envelopes)

proof_summary: COMPANY-001 defines the LifeOS-native company governance and operating model for Element Ark Holdings as a definition-only task family. generated/task_families/COMPANY-001.json enumerates the complete governed universe — eleven company objects and workflows drawn 1:1 from the task goal: (A) company identity (Element Ark Holdings conglomerate frame, David as Owner/Founder/Human Principal, MVP1 financial+legal), (B) authority (the David -> Constitution -> Board -> NOA/CECCA -> Executives -> Departments -> Workers -> Tools chain, operationalized by the 02_AUTHORITY_GRAPH.md v0 narrow graph), (C) priorities (max 3 active, 1-8 Master Plan sequence), (D) projects (priority-linked, gate-bearing, verifier-gated), (E) departments (chartered mandates, full org chart deferred post-v0), (F) roles (CECCA-bound agent+capability, authority-integrity checked), (G) decisions (bounded, unblock+rollback, CEO-proposes-not-executes), (H) budgets (forecast-only Resource Burn envelopes, David-only spend), (I) risk (ATAS/simulation constraints and the Raw-Event -> Failure-Capsule -> Causal-Map -> Scoped-Lesson -> Guardrail -> Instinct chain, MinCut isolation), (J) compliance (Runtime-Kernel constitutional enforcement plus SHAKE256 witness-chain anti-bluffing), and (K) proof workflows (the §18 pipeline, append-only proof_ledger.jsonl, verifier-gated completion). Each object is assigned exactly once the full gate clause set: a single accountable owner, an explicit authority path rooted in David, a bounded decision, an approval gate with the §11 Needs-Human-Approval triggers, a concrete evidence surface, and a rollback-or-non-action rule. The gate's terminal clause — no agent can self-authorize — is satisfied per-object via self_authorization_prevented clauses and backstopped by five cross-cutting invariants (task cannot self-verify; no unlimited authority; CEO agent proposes/delegates but does not execute; David is final human authority; recommendation may not bypass proof). Store authority aligns with the STORE-001 keystone: durable company records are PostgreSQL+RuVector canonical, transient arbitration/simulation state is redb, per-agent cognition is AgentDB (.rvf), secrets stay in the OS keyring — and STORE-001's single-durable-writer invariant mirrors the single-accountable-approver governance invariant. No organizational action, spend, legal commitment, account creation, product implementation, or secret access was performed; this is planning definition only, held within the phase-7 policy-cell boundary.
